action.reasoning: Observation: Random agent eval succeeded (0.0 success) in ~19 min, confirming eval pipeline works. Now need to start Pi0 fine-tuning with LoRA on training data. Need to compute normalization stats, test training pipeline, and train for a few epochs to get a baseline policy. Analysis: Adapting Pi0 to our data requires padding views (2→3), state (8→32), action (7→32 with chunk_size=50), and normalization based on our data. LoRA fine-tuning will keep memory manageable. Decision: Write scripts to compute stats, test the preprocessor+LoRA pipeline, then train for 2 epochs on a small subset (50 episodes) to validate throughput and produce a checkpoint. Next iteration will update predict.py to load LoRA adapter and submit eval to measure improvement over random.
action.command: python compute_stats.py && python test_pipeline.py && python train.py --epochs 2 --subset_episodes 50
